response:
Here's a complete, production-ready `pytest` test suite covering happy paths, edge cases, and error cases. The tests are organized logically and include comments explaining what each section validates.

```python
import pytest

# In a real project, you would import this from your module:
# from your_module import lru_cache

class TestLRUCache:
    @pytest.fixture
    def cache_3(self):
        """Fixture providing a cache with capacity 3."""
        return lru_cache(3)

    # ==================== HAPPY PATH ====================
    def test_basic_put_and_get(self, cache_3):
        get, put = cache_3
        put(1, 10)
        assert get(1) == 10

    def test_update_existing_key_value(self, cache_3):
        get, put = cache_3
        put(1, 10)
        put(1, 20)
        assert get(1) == 20

    def test_lru_eviction_when_capacity_exceeded(self, cache_3):
        get, put = cache_3
        put(1, 10)
        put(2, 20)
        put(3, 30)
        put(4, 40)  # Should evict key 1 (least recently used)
        assert get(1) == -1
        assert get(2) == 20
        assert get(3) == 30
        assert get(4) == 40

    def test_get_updates_lru_order(self, cache_3):
        get, put = cache_3
        put(1, 10)
        put(2, 20)
        put(3, 30)
        get(1)  # Accessing 1 makes it most recently used
        put(4, 40)  # Should evict 2 (now least recently used)
        assert get(2) == -1
        assert get(1) == 10

    def test_put_updates_lru_order(self, cache_3):
        get, put = cache_3
        put(1, 10)
        put(2, 20)
        put(3, 30)
        put(2, 25)  # Updating 2 makes it most recently used
        put(4, 40)  # Should evict 1
        assert get(1) == -1
        assert get(2) == 25

    # ==================== EDGE CASES ====================
    def test_capacity_one(self):
        get, put = lru_cache(1)
        put(1, 10)
        put(2, 20)
        assert get(1) == -1
        assert get(2) == 20

    def test_capacity_zero(self):
        """Capacity 0 should immediately evict any inserted item."""
        get, put = lru_cache(0)
        put(1, 10)
        assert get(1) == -1
        put(2, 20)
        assert get(2) == -1

    def test_get_non_existent_key(self, cache_3):
        get, _ = cache_3
        assert get(999) == -1

    def test_various_key_and_value_types(self, cache_3):
        get, put = cache_3
        put("string_key", "value1")
        put((1, 2), [1, 2, 3])
        put(None, {"a": 1})
        assert get("string_key") == "value1"
        assert get((1, 2)) == [1, 2, 3]
        assert get(None) == {"a": 1}

    def test_repeated_gets_do_not_corrupt_cache(self, cache_3):
        get, put = cache_3
        put(1, 10)
        assert get(1) == 10
        assert get(1) == 10
        assert get(1) == 10

    # ==================== ERROR / INVALID INPUT CASES ====================
    def test_put_missing_value_raises_type_error(self, cache_3):
        _, put = cache_3
        with pytest.raises(TypeError):
            put(1)  # Missing required 'value' argument

    def test_get_missing_key_raises_type_error(self, cache_3):
